title: Family, Identity, and History Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Angry and ready to fight or argue.
choices: belligerent
vindictive
narcissistic
rounds.answer: belligerent
rounds.explanation: belligerent: Angry and ready to fight or argue.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A difference or disagreement between two things that should be the same.
choices: belligerent
vindictive
discrepancy
rounds.answer: discrepancy
rounds.explanation: discrepancy: A difference or disagreement between two things that should be the same.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Showing a lack of respect or being rude about someone or something.
choices: vindictive
derogatory
belligerent
rounds.answer: derogatory
rounds.explanation: derogatory: Showing a lack of respect or being rude about someone or something.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The wish to do evil or harm to others.
rounds.answer: malevolence

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To make a problem or situation worse.
rounds.answer: exacerbate

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To make someone angry or upset by opposing them or being mean.
rounds.answer: antagonize

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: The belligerent dog barked loudly at anyone who came near.
My friends often argue about which movie to watch.
There was a conflict between the two teams during the game.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: A difference or disagreement between two things that should be the same.
A disagreement or fight between people or groups.
A situation that is difficult to deal with or understand.
To talk about something in a way that shows you have different opinions and want to convince someone.
rounds.answer: To talk about something in a way that shows you have different opinions and want to convince someone.
rounds.explanation: My friends often argue about which movie to watch.

choices: A difference or disagreement between two things that should be the same.
A discussion where people express different opinions about something.
A situation that is difficult to deal with or understand.
To make a problem or situation worse.
rounds.answer: A discussion where people express different opinions about something.
rounds.explanation: The two friends had an argument about which movie to watch.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.target: The belligerent dog barked loudly at anyone who came near.
My friends often argue about which movie to watch.
Explain one important unit idea, give a reason with because, and respond to two follow-up questions.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The mendacious story he told made everyone doubt his honesty.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The defiant student refused to follow the teacher's instructions.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: If you keep teasing your friend, you might antagonize them.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'devious' mean in this unit?
choices: Not honest or straightforward; tricky or sneaky.
Angry and ready to fight or argue.
Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
rounds.answer: Not honest or straightforward; tricky or sneaky.
rounds.explanation: devious: Not honest or straightforward; tricky or sneaky.

rounds.prompt: What does 'harass' mean in this unit?
choices: Angry and ready to fight or argue.
Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
To bother or annoy someone repeatedly.
rounds.answer: To bother or annoy someone repeatedly.
rounds.explanation: harass: To bother or annoy someone repeatedly.

rounds.prompt: What does 'vindictive' mean in this unit?
choices: Someone who is very interested in themselves and thinks they are better than others.
Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
Angry and ready to fight or argue.
rounds.answer: Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
rounds.explanation: vindictive: Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

pairs: Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
Someone who is very interested in themselves and thinks they are better than others.

games.title: Question Quest

Someone who is very interested in themselves and thinks they are better than others.
To make someone very angry.
To talk about something in a way that shows you have different opinions and want to convince someone.

rounds.explanation: It can infuriate me when people talk during a movie.
